name: The Farmers National Bank of Lebanon
description_short: Community bank in Lebanon, Kentucky serving Marion County for over 130 years with personal and business banking, digital services, and lending products.
description_long: Farmers National Bank of Lebanon has operated continuously since 1890, making it a longstanding financial institution in Marion County, Kentucky. The bank explicitly positions itself as a community-focused alternative to larger regional and national banks, emphasizing personal relationships and local knowledge over institutional scale. The bank maintains two physical locations in Lebanon and offers comprehensive banking services designed to serve both individual consumers and business owners. The bank's service offerings include individual banking (checking and savings accounts), business banking with dedicated financial services, digital banking accessible via mobile app or web portal, and various loan products. Their loan services appear to encompass personal loans, business loans, mortgages, and investment property financing based on customer testimonials. The bank also provides mortgage specialist services with an emphasis on streamlined home-buying processes. Digital banking is available across multiple platforms including dedicated mobile applications and browser-based access. Farmers National Bank distinguishes itself through emphasis on relationship banking and personalized service. Customer testimonials highlight loan officers and mortgage specialists who "go above and beyond," staff members known by name, and a commitment to making customers feel valued. The bank was voted "Best Bank in Marion County" in December 2024 across multiple categories (Best Checking/Savings, Best Loans/Lending, Best Mortgages, and Best Place to Work). Leadership changes in February 2025 included the promotion of Misha Wilson to Executive Vice President of Lending, suggesting organizational stability and growth. As a community bank, Farmers National Bank serves a specific geographic and demographic market. The primary limitation is geographic scope—the bank operates only in Lebanon, Kentucky with two locations, making it inaccessible to consumers outside Marion County except through digital channels. The website provides limited transparency regarding specific loan terms, APRs, minimum deposit requirements, or fee schedules. Customers seeking the widest product range or most competitive rates nationally may find larger institutions more suitable, though local customers prioritizing relationship banking appear highly satisfied.
pros: Over 130 years of continuous operation in Marion County with deep community roots and institutional stability
Voted Best Bank in Marion County (December 2024) across multiple categories including mortgages, lending, and checking/savings
Personalized service model with staff who know customers by name, emphasized in multiple testimonials
Digital banking available via mobile app and web portal for remote account management
Comprehensive loan offerings including personal loans, business loans, mortgages, and investment property financing
Mortgage specialists who handle application process completely, reducing customer burden per testimonials
Recent executive promotion (Misha Wilson to EVP of Lending in Feb 2025) suggests leadership commitment to lending services
cons: Limited geographic availability with only two physical locations in Lebanon, Kentucky; not accessible to non-local customers except digitally
Website lacks transparent pricing information including specific APRs, loan terms, minimum deposits, or fee schedules
No information about loan approval timelines, credit score requirements, or underwriting standards
Limited digital features described on website—unclear if mobile app includes loan application, investment services, or other advanced features
Small institution may have less competitive rates than larger regional or national banks
best_for: Marion County residents seeking relationship-based banking with local decision-makers and staff who know them personally
Small business owners needing business banking and commercial lending with personalized service
First-time homebuyers in the Lebanon area who want mortgage specialists to guide them through the process
Customers prioritizing community investment and local financial institution support over national bank convenience
diagnosis: Farmers National Bank is best for Marion County residents and business owners who value relationship banking, local decision-making, and personalized service from staff who know them by name. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—the bank operates only in Lebanon, Kentucky with two physical locations, making it impractical for customers outside Marion County seeking in-person services, though digital banking provides some remote access.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
fdic_official_name: The Farmers National Bank of Lebanon
